genre_preference.value: documentary, action, true stories, romantic comedy
evidence: "A very good and thorough documentary of Janis Joplin but a sad story to say the least."
"I remember watching this movie years ago. The car/train chase is one of a few classic car chases in cinema."
"A believeable fictional account of the drug trade battles along our border."
"The movie plot was just too slow and boring. Great actors and story had potential but moved too slow for me to sit and finish movie."
"A frustrating, suspenseful, true account of what happened in Benghazi."
"Well written tale with a wonderful cast. It was my second time seeing it. I recommend it."
"Anyone who is a Clapton fan will enjoy this documentary. Been a fan for years and thoroughly enjoyed it."
"It saddens me to think with all the success and fame one achieves in life it can never fill the emptiness of a heart broken by either family or the world."
"The movie makes you think that just maybe one or more frustrating moments in our day may actually be blessings or fate in disguise."
"A very good and thorough documentary of Janis Joplin but a sad story to say the least."
"An inspirational story of students beating the odds."

summary: The user enjoys documentaries and true stories, particularly those with emotional depth and strong narratives. They prefer fast-paced films with high production quality and appreciate well-acted performances, while showing a tendency towards simple storytelling.
